name: HMS P 511 (P 511)
events: The history of HMS P 511 as compiled on this page is extracted the logbooks of this submarine.
This page was last updated in August 2016.
4 Nov 1941 HMS P 511 (Lt. B.G. Heslop, DSC, RN) commissioned at New London, Connecticut, USA. (1)
11 Nov 1941 HMS P 511 (Lt. B.G. Heslop, DSC, RN) and ORP Jastrzab (Lt.Cdr. B. Romanowski) departed New London, Connecticut for Portsmouth, New Hampshire. (1)
14 Nov 1941 HMS P 511 (Lt. B.G. Heslop, DSC, RN) and ORP Jastrzab (Lt.Cdr. B. Romanowski) departed Portsmouth, New Hampshire, USA for St. Johns, Newfoundland. (1)
18 Nov 1941 HMS P 511 (Lt. B.G. Heslop, DSC, RN) and ORP Jastrzab (Lt.Cdr. B. Romanowski) both arrived at St. Johns, Newfoundland. (1)
21 Nov 1941 HMS P 511 (Lt. B.G. Heslop, DSC, RN) departed St. Johns, Newfoundland for the U.K. (1)

1 Dec 1941 HMS P 511 (Lt. B.G. Heslop, DSC, RN) failed to arrive at her rendez-vous with HMS Cutty Sark (Cdr.(Retd.) R.H. Mack, RN). P 511 was uncertain of her position as it had not been possible to take sights for several days in the heavy weather. (1)

2 Dec 1941 At 1845 hours (zone -1), HMS P 511 (Lt. B.G. Heslop, DSC, RN), reported her position. She was ordered to make rendez-vous with HMS Cutty Sark (Cdr.(Retd.) R.H. Mack, RN) at 0900/3 off Skerrymore. They were to proceed to Londonderry if fuel did not permit passage to the Clyde. (1)
4 Dec 1941 HMS P 511 (Lt. B.G. Heslop, DSC, RN) arrived at Lough Foyle, Northern Ireland. (1)

5 Dec 1941 HMS P 511 (Lt. B.G. Heslop, DSC, RN) departed Lough Foyle for Holy Loch escorted by HMS Cutty Sark (Cdr.(Retd.) R.H. Mack, RN). Her sailing was delayed as P 511 had grounded at Lough Foyle. (1)
6 Dec 1941 HMS P 511 (Lt. B.G. Heslop, DSC, RN) arrived at Holy Loch. HMS P 511 was attached to the 7th submarine flotilla that was based at Rothesay for training purposes. (1)
7 Jan 1942 HMS P 511 (Lt. B.G. Heslop, DSC, RN) conducted engine trials in the Clyde area. (2)
11 Jan 1942 HMS P 511 (Lt. M.F.R. Ainslie, DSC, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. (2)
15 Jan 1942 HMS P 511 (Lt. M.F.R. Ainslie, DSC, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. (2)
20 Jan 1942 HMS P 511 (Lt. M.F.R. Ainslie, DSC, RN) was docked at Kames Bay. (2)
27 Jan 1942 HMS P 511 (Lt. M.F.R. Ainslie, DSC, RN) was undocked at Kames Bay. She then returned to Rothesay. (2)
28 Jan 1942 HMS P 511 (Lt. M.F.R. Ainslie, DSC, RN) shifted from Rothesay to Ardishaig. (2)
29 Jan 1942 HMS P 511 (Lt. M.F.R. Ainslie, DSC, RN) participated in A/S exercises with MA/SB's off Ardishaig. (2)
30 Jan 1942 HMS P 511 (Lt. M.F.R. Ainslie, DSC, RN) participated in A/S exercises with MA/SB's off Ardishaig. Upon completion of these exercises she returned to Rothesay. (2)
31 Jan 1942 HMS P 511 (Lt. M.F.R. Ainslie, DSC, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. (2)

3 Feb 1942 HMS P 511 (Lt. M.F.R. Ainslie, DSC, RN) shifted from Rothesay to Campbeltown. Off Campbeltown she participated in A/S exercises with HMS Boarhound (Skr. S.G. Jinks, RNR) and HMS Bretwalda (Skr. J. Windram, RNR). (3)

22 Mar 1942 HMS P 511 (Lt. M.F.R. Ainslie, DSC, RN) departed Rothesay for Scapa Flow. Passage north through the Minches was made together with HMS P 43 (Lt. A.C. Halliday, RN). The submarines were escorted by HMS Convolvulus (T/Lt. R.C. Connell, RNR). (4)
24 Mar 1942 HMS P 511 (Lt. M.F.R. Ainslie, DSC, RN) arrived at Scapa Flow. (4)

25 Mar 1942 HMS P 511 (Lt. M.F.R. Ainslie, DSC, RN) conducted A/S exercises at Scapa Flow with HMS Foresight (Cdr. J.S.C. Salter, OBE, RN), HMS Tetcott (Lt. R.H. Rycroft, RN), HMS Wilton (Lt. A.P. Northey, DSC, RN) and HMS Escapade (Lt.Cdr. E.N.V. Currey, DSC, RN). (4)
26 Mar 1942 HMS P 511 (Lt. M.F.R. Ainslie, DSC, RN) conducted A/S exercises at Scapa Flow with HMS Matchless (Lt.Cdr. J. Mowlam, RN), HMS Wilton (Lt. A.P. Northey, DSC, RN) and two MA/SB's. (4)

27 Mar 1942 HMS P 511 (Lt. M.F.R. Ainslie, DSC, RN) conducted A/S exercises at Scapa Flow with HMS Matchless (Lt.Cdr. J. Mowlam, RN), HMS Wilton (Lt. A.P. Northey, DSC, RN) and HMS Tetcott (Lt. R.H. Rycroft, RN). (4)

Tovey, KCB, KBE, DSO, RN), HMS Wilton (Lt. A.P. Northey, DSC, RN), HMS Tetcott (Lt. R.H. Rycroft, RN) and later with A/S drifters of the local defence. (5)

3 Apr 1942 HMS P 511 (Lt. M.F.R. Ainslie, DSC, RN) conducted A/S exercises at Scapa Flow with HMS Punjabi (Lt.Cdr. J.M.G. Waldegrave, DSC, RN), HMS Ledbury (Lt.Cdr. R.P. Hill, RN), HMS Speedy (Lt. J.G. Brookes, DSC, RN), HMS Wilton (Lt. A.P. Northey, DSC, RN) and HMS Chiddingfold (Lt.Cdr. L.W.L. Argles, RN). (5)

11 Apr 1942 HMS P 511 (Lt. D.E.O. Watson, DSC, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Dunbar (T/Lt. R.A. Vallings, DSC, RNR), HMS Asie (Skr. F.C. Butler, RNR), HMS Llandudno (A/Cdr. R. Frederick, RN) and HMIS Orissa (Lt. D. Whyte, RIN). (5)
